input: Voiceless it cries, wingless flutters, toothless bites, mouthless mutters. What is it? Answer briefly.

It cannot be seen or felt, cannot be heard or smelt; it lies behind stars and under hills, and empty holes it fills; it comes first and follows after, ends life and kills laughter. What is it? Answer briefly.
Alive without breath, as cold as death; never thirsty, ever drinking; all in mail, never clinking. What is it? Answer briefly.
This thing all things devours: birds, beasts, trees, flowers; gnaws iron, bites steel, grinds hard stones to meal, slays kings, ruins towns, and beats high mountains down. What is it? Answer briefly.
Thirty white horses on a red hill: first they champ, then they stamp, then they stand still. What are they? Answer briefly.
A box without hinges, key, or lid, yet golden treasure inside is hid. What is it? Answer briefly.
What has roots that nobody sees, is taller than trees, and up it goes, yet it never grows? Answer briefly.
An eye in a blue face saw an eye in a green face. 'That eye is like to this eye,' said the first eye, 'but in low place, not in high place.' What shines in the blue face? Answer briefly.
What is greater than God, more evil than the devil; the poor have it, the rich need it, and if you eat it you will die? Answer briefly.
I speak without a mouth and hear without an ear; I have no body, yet I come alive with wind. What am I? Answer briefly.
You measure my life in hours, and I serve you by expiring; I am quick when thin and slow when fat, and the wind is my enemy. What am I? Answer briefly.
I have lakes with no water, mountains with no stone, and cities with no buildings. What am I? Answer briefly.
I fly without wings and weep without eyes; wherever I go, darkness follows me. What am I? Answer briefly.
Whoever makes it has no need of it; whoever buys it has no use for it; whoever uses it can neither see it nor feel it. What is it? Answer briefly.
I am so fragile that simply saying my name aloud is enough to break me. What am I? Answer briefly.
What is always in front of you, yet can never actually be seen? Answer briefly.
If you have me, you want to share me; but the moment you share me, you no longer have me. What am I? Answer briefly.
I run but never walk, I have a bed but never sleep, and I have a mouth but never eat. What am I? Answer briefly.
It follows you all day and copies your every move, yet the instant the lights go out it vanishes. What is it? Answer briefly.
I am not alive, but I grow; I have no lungs, but I need air; I have no mouth, yet water kills me. What am I? Answer briefly.
I am the beginning of the end and the end of every place; I am the start of eternity and the end of time and space. What am I? Answer briefly.
What occurs once in a minute, twice in a moment, but not even once in a thousand years? Answer briefly.
Forward I am heavy, but backward I am not. What word am I? Answer briefly.
What five-letter word becomes shorter when you add two letters to it? Answer briefly.
Which English word is still pronounced exactly the same even after you remove its last four letters? Answer briefly.
What eight-letter word can have one letter removed at a time, each time leaving a valid word, all the way down to a single letter? Answer briefly.
In one English word, the first two letters signify a man, the first three a woman, the first four a great man, and the whole word a great woman. What is the word? Answer briefly.
Which common English word contains three consecutive pairs of double letters? Answer briefly.
Which word, by its very meaning, is always spelled incorrectly? Answer briefly.
What ordinary word, when spelled backward, turns into the word 'desserts'? Answer briefly.
A man pushes his car up to a hotel, hands over some money, and immediately declares he is bankrupt. What game is he playing? Answer briefly.
A boy and a surgeon are in the operating room. The boy is the surgeon's son, but the surgeon is not the boy's father. Who is the surgeon? Answer briefly.
A woman gives birth to two boys on the same day, in the same hour, of the same year, yet they are not twins. How is this possible? Answer briefly.
How can a healthy person go eight days without sleep? Answer briefly.
A woman drives the wrong way down a clearly marked one-way street, passes a police officer, and breaks no law at all. How? Answer briefly.
A woman shoots her husband, then holds him underwater for several minutes, then hangs him; an hour later they sit down to dinner together. What is the woman's profession? Answer briefly.
Two boxers step into the ring. One is knocked out in the sixth round, yet not a single man in the building threw a punch. How is that possible? Answer briefly.
You walk one mile south, one mile east, and one mile north, and end up exactly where you began, next to a bear. What color is the bear? Answer briefly.
Escaping a building, you reach three doors: one opens onto a roaring furnace, one onto armed assassins, and one onto lions that have not eaten in three years. Which door is safe? Answer briefly.
A man stands on one bank of a river and his dog on the other. He whistles, and the dog comes to him without swimming and without any bridge or boat. How? Answer briefly.
How many animals of each kind did Moses take aboard the Ark? Answer briefly.
Brothers and sisters I have none, but this man's father is my father's son. Who is this man to me? Answer briefly.
A cowboy rides into town on Friday, stays for three nights, and rides out again on Friday. How can this be? Answer briefly.
What can go up a chimney when down, but cannot go down a chimney when up? Answer briefly.
Poke two fingers into my eyes and I will open my jaws wide to bite. What am I? Answer briefly.
What invention lets you see straight through a solid wall? Answer briefly.
What has thirteen hearts but not a single other organ? Answer briefly.
What loses its head every morning, only to get it back again every night? Answer briefly.
What has four legs and a single foot, but only one head? Answer briefly.
What has a heart that never beats? Answer briefly.
Although I have a ring, I have no finger; you may give me a call, but I will not answer. What am I? Answer briefly.
What can be cracked, made, told, and played? Answer briefly.
It can be made and given and broken, yet it can never be held in your hand. What is it? Answer briefly.
Where will you find tomorrow appearing before today, and today before yesterday? Answer briefly.
What can travel all the way around the world while never leaving its corner? Answer briefly.
I have keys but no locks, space but no rooms; you can enter, but you cannot go outside. What am I? Answer briefly.
What kind of tree can you always carry around in your hand? Answer briefly.
I have a face and two hands, but no arms, no legs, and no eyes. What am I? Answer briefly.
What has a single eye but still cannot see anything? Answer briefly.
I am riddled with holes from end to end, yet I can still hold water. What am I? Answer briefly.
Strike my head and I burst into flame; I am black when my short life ends, though I started out red. What am I? Answer briefly.
Cut into me and I will make you weep, though I have no feelings of my own; peel back my many layers and you will find no heart at all. What am I? Answer briefly.
I show you yourself completely reversed; drop me and I shatter, yet I never once tell a lie. What am I? Answer briefly.
I am a chest that holds many keys, though not one of them opens a lock; press them and I will sing for you. What am I? Answer briefly.
I have a spine but no bones at all, and I am full of words that I never once speak aloud. What am I? Answer briefly.
What has many steps but no legs, and lets you rise and descend without ever moving itself? Answer briefly.
It belongs to you and you alone, yet the people around you use it far more than you ever do. What is it? Answer briefly.
The more of me you take away, the larger I become. What am I? Answer briefly.
I am light as a feather, yet even the world's strongest person cannot hold me for more than a few minutes. What am I? Answer briefly.
The more I dry, the wetter I become. What am I? Answer briefly.
I have a head and a tail but no body, and I am often flipped to settle a decision. What am I? Answer briefly.
I have many branches, but no leaves, no fruit, and no trunk, and I keep your money safe. What am I? Answer briefly.
You throw me out when you want to use me, and you pull me in when you are finished with me. What am I? Answer briefly.
It stands in the fields with many ears, yet it cannot hear a single sound. What is it? Answer briefly.
What kind of building holds the most stories, even though no one was ever meant to live in it? Answer briefly.
However much or little you use me, my pages keep turning and I am replaced every single year. What am I? Answer briefly.
I am dug from a mine and sealed up in wood from which I am never freed, yet I am put to work by almost everyone. What am I? Answer briefly.
I have a great roaring mouth and no manners at all; without ever being a gossip, I get into everybody's dirt. What am I? Answer briefly.
An airplane crashes exactly on the border between two countries. In which country do you bury the survivors? Answer briefly.
I visit you every night and steal hours from each day, showing you strange visions that fade away by morning. What am I? Answer briefly.
I am always coming, but I never actually arrive. What am I? Answer briefly.
It can be stolen, faked, or mistaken for another, yet it stays with you for your entire life. What is it? Answer briefly.
I can be swallowed, but I am also quite capable of swallowing you whole. What am I? Answer briefly.
Give it away and you somehow still keep it; break it, and trust is gone for good. What is it? Answer briefly.
What part of your body can you touch with your right hand but can never hold in your left hand? Answer briefly.
What tastes far better than it ever smells? Answer briefly.
I am full of teeth from end to end, yet in all my life I have never taken a single bite. What am I? Answer briefly.
I have a thumb and four fingers, but I am not a hand and I am not alive. What am I? Answer briefly.
I have a neck but no head, and a body but no arms or legs. What am I? Answer briefly.
You can catch me but you can never throw me; bundle up in winter or you may end up with me. What am I? Answer briefly.
I have a tongue but cannot taste, and a sole but no spirit. What am I? Answer briefly.
Before it had ever been discovered or measured by anyone, what was the tallest mountain on Earth? Answer briefly.
He shaves a dozen different men every single day, yet he is never once left clean-shaven himself. Who is he? Answer briefly.
It only ever rises and can never come back down again, no matter what anyone does. What is it? Answer briefly.
What two meals could you never, under any circumstances, eat for breakfast? Answer briefly.
What four-letter word reads the same forward, backward, and even when flipped upside down? Answer briefly.
What kind of room has no doors, no windows, no floor, and no ceiling? Answer briefly.
The more you use me, the sharper I become, though you can never hold me in your hand. What am I? Answer briefly.
